entries.title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
entries.description: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

entries.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
entries.description: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

entries.title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
entries.description: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

entries.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
entries.description: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

entries.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
entries.description: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

entries.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
entries.description: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

entries.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent-building tool for enterprises
entries.description: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: It is geared for IT and technical users.

entries.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
entries.description: The AI Overviews will offer instant summaries pulled from across multiple emails.

entries.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
entries.description: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

entries.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
entries.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

entries.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
entries.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

entries.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
entries.description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

entries.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
entries.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

entries.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
entries.description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

entries.title: AI galaxy hunters are adding to the global GPU crunch
entries.description: Astronomers are turning to GPUs to find needles in the galactic haystack.

entries.title: Beehiiv rolls out new creator tools, including webinars and customizable paywalls
entries.description: The announcement is a clear sign the company is trying to become an all-in-one hub for creators, reducing the hassle of juggling various tools and services to run their businesses.

entries.title: India’s app market is booming — but global platforms are capturing most of the gains
entries.description: Non-gaming apps, led by streaming and AI, are driving growth, even as India's spending per user lags global peers.

entries.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
entries.description: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.
